ops.title: capt-hook 5.0.0 renamed command.Command → ParsedCommand (pack migrated 2026-07-02); types.Command is a DIFFERENT surviving symbol; 5.0.0 mis-pins cc-transcript

ops.body:
capt-hook **5.0.0** renamed the parsed-command dataclass `captain_hook.command.Command` → `ParsedCommand`. The cc-notes hooks pack **migrated on 2026-07-02** (record.py + tests now import `ParsedCommand`; full suite 102/102 green under `capt-hook==5.0.0`) — do not reintroduce the old import, and verify against 5.0.0, not a `<5` pin.

**Traps:**
- `captain_hook.types.Command` still exists in 5.0.0 and is a *different* symbol — a regex-match condition class used by `workflow.py` in `only_if=[Command(...)]`. Only `command.Command` was renamed; a blanket rename breaks workflow.py.
- Upstream bug: capt-hook 5.0.0 imports `WorkflowCall` from `cc_transcript.tools` (exists only in cc-transcript ≥7.1.0) but declares `cc-transcript>=7,<8` — a resolver landing on 7.0.x ImportErrors at capt-hook import time. Fix pending in ~/Code/captain-hook: bump to `>=7.1` and release ([[capt-hook-is-cc-notes-hooks-upstream]] — release-gated).
- Stale uv *preference* caches can keep selecting cc-transcript==7.0.1 even with UV_EXCLUDE_NEWER cleared (the staleness lives in uv's preference cache, not the env var) — bypass with `--no-cache`/`--refresh`/`--upgrade-package cc-transcript`; a full `uv cache clean` needs the resident semble MCP uvx processes stopped (they hold the cache lock). Related: [[uv-exclude-newer-hides-fresh-publishes]].
